Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Lyathatan]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 3''' — The Lyathatan is a giant creature that pulls Calder's ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 5''' — The Lyathatan is a ship that can anchor in the Aion.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 5''' — The Lyathatan is a ship or entity Calder can command.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 7''' — The Lyathatan is the ship Calder is on.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 7''' — The Lyathatan is a ship with chains and an awareness that can be read.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 9''' — Calder Read from the Lyathatan to discover the island's shape.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 9''' — Lyathatan are creatures that live alongside Calder.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 11''' — The Lyathatan hails from Kelarac.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 12''' — The Lyathatan is a creature or entity that could match the Children of the Dead Mother.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 12''' — The Lyathatan is drifting beneath the island.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 16''' — The Lyathatan is a location or entity with a shadow that writhes beneath the water.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 16''' — Calder mentions feeding themselves to the Lyathatan.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 18''' — The Lyathatan has blue, six-fingered, webbed hands.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 22''' — A Lyathatan is something people can have a link to.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 23''' — Calder left orders for the Lyathatan to take his ship around to the back of the island.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 23''' — Calder Marten can barely feel the Lyathatan.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan is a huge creature shaped like a man that pulls the ship The Testament.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan has fish-like scales and a spine with ridges.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan has six-fingered hands that are webbed and tipped with spike-like nails.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan has a shark's mouth and three black eyes on either side of its head.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan has gills on its short, muscular neck.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan is an Elderspawn.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan is a being of indescribable age and vast hunger.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan is in servitude to Calder.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 25''' — A deeper link to the Lyathatan is required for help.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 25''' — The Lyathatan has a hand with six clawed, double-jointed fingers.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 25''' — The Lyathatan has a deep and fathomless mind.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 25''' — The Lyathatan is a being with passion in its expression.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 26''' — A Lyathatan can be used to pull a ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|'''Ch 27''' — Kelarac enhanced Calder's ability to communicate with the Lyathatan.}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 2''' — The Lyathatan has deep blue scales, six black eyes in two rows of three, and a shark-like mouth with jagged teeth.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 2''' — The Lyathatan has a torso like a man's and muscular arms with taloned hands.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 2''' — The Lyathatan is described as an Elder-spawned echo of a mermaid.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 2''' — The Lyathatan is an Elderspawn with six black eyes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 3''' — The voice asked if they could capture the Lyathatan.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 4''' — The Lyathatan is a being that Calder sent his Intent down to.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 5''' — The appearance of the Lyathatan delighted Jerri.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 5''' — The Lyathatan is a bound Elder sworn into service by invested chains.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 6''' — Calder uses Reading to communicate with the Lyathatan.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 11''' — The Lyathatan is an Elder that Calder can Read.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 13''' — The Lyathatan is a creature that looks like it is made of sharks and nightmares.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 19''' — Calder originally planned to let the Lyathatan deal with the whale-sized shadow.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 20''' — The Lyathatan is a ship that drifts sluggishly beneath The Testament.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 21''' — A Reader might be able to persuade the Lyathatan to move.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 23''' — The Lyathatan is a servant of Kelarac.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|'''Ch 23''' — The Lyathatan is a vessel that can slow to a crawl.}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 4''' — The Lyathatan is a ship that rests on the sea floor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 14''' — The Lyathatan cleared the waters around the ship per its agreement.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 16''' — The Lyathatan is a ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 16''' — The Lyathatan has manacles.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 19''' — The Testament is under heavy guard due to fear of the Lyathatan.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 20''' — The Lyathatan is an Elder creature bound to the hull of Calder's ship by chains.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 22''' — The Lyathatan is wading through the ocean and dragging a ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan is an Elder that Calder can mentally order.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 24''' — The Lyathatan is a creature that was shirking its duties.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 25''' — The Lyathatan is a creature with six black eyes that burst from the Aion Sea.}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|'''Ch 25''' — The Lyathatan was snarling and cupping its missing hand.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
